PATTERN.title: TDD in .meta.tests_sandbox
PATTERN.context: When implementing new features or fixing bugs in Agent-X
PATTERN.finding: Tests must be written before code in .meta.tests_sandbox/ following Kent Beck methodology
PATTERN.solution: 1. Write failing test in .meta.tests_sandbox/ 2. Implement minimum code in .meta.sandbox/ 3. Run tests 4. Refactor

PATTERN.title: Never modify production code directly
PATTERN.finding: Production code must never be modified without going through safe spaces
PATTERN.solution: Always work in .meta.sandbox/ for code changes, .meta.tests_sandbox/ for tests, .meta.experiments/ for prototypes

PATTERN.title: Standalone tools with no dependencies
PATTERN.finding: Tools should work without external dependencies when possible
PATTERN.solution: Use stdlib only, make optional dependencies truly optional, provide fallbacks

FINDING.title: Use uv for dependency management
FINDING.context: When adding Python packages to Agent-X
FINDING.finding: uv is faster and more reliable than pip, and is the project standard
FINDING.solution: Always use 'uv add package' instead of 'pip install' or 'pip'

DECISION.title: Meta Project Harness structure
DECISION.finding: Safe spaces (.meta.*) separate production from development work
DECISION.solution: Work in .meta.sandbox/, .meta.experiments/, or .meta.tests_sandbox/ based on task type
